basic_profile.legal_system: Federal republic, common law with significant state-level variation

capital_controls.value: The US does not generally operate a Colombia-style exchange-control regime, but transfers remain subject to sanctions, AML/BSA, tax, and reporting rules

general_attitude_bitcoin.value: Legal to own; 2025 federal posture moved toward institutional integration; defining tension is reporting, not legality

tax_residence_basis.value: Citizens and green-card holders taxed on worldwide income regardless of residence; residents taxed on worldwide income; non-residents on US-source income

capital_gains_treatment.value: Digital assets including Bitcoin treated as property; disposals are taxable events reported on Form 8949 and Schedule D; holding period sets short vs long term

bitcoin_classification.value: Property (not currency) for federal income tax

treatment_btc_to_btc.value: Disposition of property; generally a taxable event

treatment_btc_to_stablecoin.value: A Bitcoin-to-stablecoin trade is generally treated as an exchange/disposition of digital-asset property and may create gain or loss

treatment_btc_payments.value: Paying with Bitcoin is a disposition; gain is taxable

treatment_lightning.value: No Lightning-specific IRS guidance found this pass; treat as property disposition by default

treatment_btc_loans.value: No Bitcoin-specific federal guidance found; general loan/property principles would apply

treatment_etf_vs_self_custody.value: Both taxable as property on disposition; difference is reporting (broker forms vs self-reporting)

self_custody_allowed.value: Yes; FinCEN guidance FIN-2019-G001 treats non-transmitting unhosted-wallet software providers as not money transmitters; 2020 unhosted-wallet proposal withdrawn in 2024

funds_etfs_allowed.value: Yes; spot Bitcoin ETPs trade on US national exchanges (SEC approved Jan 10, 2024)

stablecoins_regulated.value: The GENIUS Act, enacted 18 July 2025 (Public Law 119-27), created a federal US payment-stablecoin framework; implementation details depend on rulemaking and effective-date provisions

btc_vs_broader_crypto.value: Rules often address 'digital assets' broadly; security-vs-commodity disputes bear more on other tokens; do not assume token rulings apply to Bitcoin

broker_reporting.value: Form 1099-DA: gross proceeds for transactions on/after 2026-01-01... see note
broker_reporting.note: Gross proceeds for transactions on or after Jan 1 2025; adjusted basis for certain covered transactions on or after Jan 1 2026; backup-withholding relief extended to Jan 1 2027 (Notice 2025-33).

crs_participation.value: US is NOT a CRS participating jurisdiction; relies on FATCA
crs_participation.note: Sourced to the OECD CRS-by-jurisdiction list (US absent). Confirm URL resolves at next review.

carf_status.value: The US has implemented domestic digital-asset broker reporting through Form 1099-DA; its CARF participation/coordination status should be separately confirmed

fatf_travel_rule.value: Implemented via FinCEN funds-transfer/travel rule for MSBs/VASPs (commonly cited $3,000 threshold)

foreign_account_reporting.value: FBAR (>$10k aggregate) and Form 8938 apply; a foreign account holding ONLY virtual currency is currently not FBAR-reportable, but FinCEN noticed intent to amend; not finalized as of this review

beneficial_ownership_registry.value: Corporate Transparency Act BOI reporting to FinCEN; scope shifted in 2024-2025; current applicability needs sourcing

forced_heirship.value: Most US states generally allow broad testamentary freedom, subject to spouse/elective-share and other state-law limits; Louisiana has a limited forced-heirship regime

estate_tax.value: Federal lifetime estate/gift exemption $15,000,000 per individual for 2026 (up from $13.99M in 2025) under 2025 tax law; portability between spouses; several states levy their own estate/inheritance taxes with lower thresholds

gift_tax.value: Annual gift exclusion $19,000 per recipient for 2026

trust_recognition.value: Trusts are a core, well-recognized US planning vehicle

probate_privacy.value: Probate is generally a public court process; revocable trusts commonly used to avoid probate and preserve privacy

digital_asset_guidance.value: Most states adopted a RUFADAA variant governing fiduciary access to digital accounts; does not solve technical key access for self-custodied Bitcoin

authority_vs_access.value: An executor/trustee can hold full legal authority and still be unable to move self-custodied Bitcoin without the keys; this is the central estate gap

citizenship_obligations.value: Citizens and green-card holders remain US-taxable wherever they live; renouncing does not automatically end exposure

exit_tax.value: Section 877A deems a sale of worldwide property the day before expatriation for a 'covered expatriate'; covered if net worth >= $2,000,000, or 5-yr avg annual net income tax > $206,000 (2025), or failure to certify 5 years compliance on Form 8854; gain exclusion $890,000 (2025)

accidental_tax_residence_risk.value: Substantial-presence test can make a non-citizen a US tax resident by day count

passport_vs_residence_vs_tax_vs_domicile.value: US passport, green card (residence), tax residence, and state domicile are distinct and can attach independently

exchange_custody.value: Most reportable and legible (1099 forms); lowest self-custody burden, highest counterparty/reporting visibility

inheritance.value: Legal authority does not equal key access; dominant planning gap for self-custody

moving_residence.value: Citizenship-based taxation and the exit tax make leaving the US a tax event to plan, not a clean exit

retirement_structure.value: Bitcoin can be held in certain retirement structures; rules are specific and custodian-dependent

direction_of_travel.label: reporting-heavy
direction_of_travel.rationale: 1099-DA phase-in increases reporting for on-exchange activity
direction_of_travel.counterevidence: Self-custody-to-self-custody activity remains comparatively unreported; 2020 unhosted-wallet proposal withdrawn

direction_of_travel.label: institution-friendly
direction_of_travel.counterevidence: Institutional integration does not reduce individual reporting

direction_of_travel.label: self-custody-friendly
direction_of_travel.rationale: Self-custody is legal; unhosted-wallet proposal withdrawn
direction_of_travel.counterevidence: Travel-rule and reporting pressure on on-ramps/off-ramps

open_questions.question: Stablecoin framework scope and effective dates
CARF commitment status and US adoption timeline
Corporate Transparency Act BOI applicability after 2024-2025 changes
State estate/inheritance tax and forced-heirship specifics (Louisiana, community-property states)
Asset-protection/seizure section needs dedicated Tier-1 sourcing before publication

watchlist.item: Form 1099-DA basis reporting (2026 transactions) and backup-withholding (2027)
FinCEN proposal to make foreign virtual-currency accounts FBAR-reportable

product_routing_notes: US users frequently surface tax_visibility_issue (records discipline, multi-jurisdiction) and, for self-custody holders with families, estate_mismatch (authority vs access). Citizenship-based taxation means 'considering_relocation' users almost always need professional review before moving.

change_log.change: Initial exemplar profile from Tier-1 IRS/FinCEN plus Tier-2 firm explainers for 2026 figures; Low/Unknown sections flagged with open tasks.
